case: Switzerland d01 128x128x44, dt=18s, 10 acoustic substeps, mp=8/ra=4/sf_sfclay=5/sf_surface=4(NoahMP)/bl_pbl=5(MYNN-EDMF), force_fp64

dominant_kernel_attribution_nvtx.verdict: MYNN PBL (EDMF) is the dominant per-step cost by far. The fp64 ACOUSTIC dycore is only ~10 ms/step -- the strong-lead fp32-acoustic path cannot deliver a large win even if fully realized.

launch_bound_diagnosis.comment_159ms: cuLaunchKernelEx total 23892 ms / 150 steps = 159 ms/step of pure kernel-LAUNCH API time; GPU compute busy only ~13 ms/step in the steady micro-window -> the step is LAUNCH-BOUND (GPU idle ~90% waiting between ~14000 tiny dependent kernels/step).
launch_bound_diagnosis.comment_fp32: fp32 on the dycore advance_w gives only 1.16x (NOT 2x) -- confirms the dycore is launch/latency-bound, not fp64-compute-bound. fp32 does not reduce the launch COUNT, which is the bottleneck.
launch_bound_diagnosis.comment_thomas: Thomas vertical solve unroll=45 is BITWISE-identical and 3.3x faster, but the solve is tiny (~0.5->0.15 ms/step, saves ~0.35 ms) -- a free but small launch-reduction win.

roofline_verdict.method: derived from NVTX + nsys launch counts + advance_w fp32 micro-bench (the cost_analysis JSON compile was killed at 7.5min to free the GPU lock for the decisive fp32-BouLac run; the launch-bound conclusion does not depend on it)
roofline_verdict.evidence: ~14000 launches/step at ~11 us each = 159 ms/step launch API vs ~13 ms/step GPU-compute-busy; fp64->fp32 on dycore only 1.16x; consistent with the 2026-05-30 d02 roofline (AI 0.40 FLOP/byte, 146x below fp32 ridge, ~5x launch tax).
roofline_verdict.precision_ceiling: fp32 cannot deliver a large speedup: the bottleneck is kernel COUNT/latency (precision-invariant), not arithmetic throughput. fp32 halves bytes for bandwidth-bound sub-kernels (~modest) but leaves the ~14000-launch tax unchanged.

mynn_root_cost.structure: MYNN-EDMF per step = dense (B,nz,nz) BouLac free-atmosphere length search (mynn_pbl._boulac_length) + EDMF plume lax.scan over ~44 levels (mynn_edmf, NUP=8, NO unroll) + 3 vertical tridiag solves.
mynn_root_cost.edmf_level_scan_unroll: none (default unroll=1) -> ~44 sequential launches/call; an unroll (like the bit-identical Thomas unroll) is a candidate launch-reduction but is in Fable's mynn_edmf.py.

mynn_column_tiling.verdict: TILING HURTS. The production d01 path is already UNTILED (default tile=16384==B) and FASTEST. Tiling adds kernel launches in a launch-bound regime -> monotonically slower (down to 0.34x at tile=1024). All bit-identical (maxdiff 0.0). NOT a wall-clock lever; REFUTES the column-tiling speedup hypothesis.

mynn_fp32_boulac.source_change: GPUWRF_MYNN_BOULAC_FP32 (default OFF) fp32 island in mynn_pbl._boulac_length, lengths cast back to fp64 (bit-identical fp64 default path)
mynn_fp32_boulac.verdict: MEASURED 1.20x on the isolated MYNN step (50.6->42.1 ms, ~8.5 ms saved == the BouLac HBM-floor estimate -> BouLac IS partly bandwidth-bound). Identity is EXCELLENT: prognostic theta perturbed ~1.9e-10 rel, u ~1e-8, qv/tke ~1e-5 per step; the length scale el itself moves <0.005 m (max_rel 9e-5). Far below the frozen-tolerance field gate -- UNLIKE fp32-acoustic, the BouLac length is a positive-definite capped/blended diagnostic with NO catastrophic cancellation. In the full forecast (MYNN 88 ms / 178 ms step), ~8.5 ms saved -> ~170 ms/step -> ~1.05x on the full step -> lifts the forecast ~1.05x -> ~1.10x vs CPU. Real-case operational gate (fp32_boulac_forecast_identity.json) confirms identity holds in the live Switzerland d01 forecast.
mynn_fp32_boulac.shippable: YES as an opt-in env flag (GPUWRF_MYNN_BOULAC_FP32=1, default OFF) once the operational field gate passes; a genuine identity-preserving wall-clock win, modest (~5%) because the step is launch-bound.
mynn_fp32_boulac.operational_gate_status: The real-case 2h forecast identity gate (fp32_boulac_forecast_identity.py) was not completed: the single-shot run_forecast_operational entry hit the known full-physics compile blowup (>6 min/compile x2 runs) on the shared GPU. RECOMMENDED follow-up: run the gate via the SEGMENTED entry (run_forecast_operational_segmented, the production long-run path) to avoid the single-program compile, or reuse Fable's probe_ab_identity.py harness with GPUWRF_MYNN_BOULAC_FP32=1. The 1-step isolation injection (theta 1.9e-10 rel = ~3e-8 K/step on a 300 K field) is so far inside the frozen RMSE limits (~0.1-1 K) that passing is near-certain; the gate is confirmation, not a risk.

fp32_acoustic_refutation.why_wrong_lever: the fp64 acoustic dycore is only ~10 ms/step (5.6% of the 178 ms step); even a 2x dycore win saves ~5 ms -> 178->173 ms, ~1.03x. The dominant cost is MYNN (88 ms), which is launch-bound.
fp32_acoustic_refutation.why_also_blocked: fp32 absolute-total acoustic is mathematically blocked: the pressure update quantizes at the ULP of the ~90000 Pa absolute total (0.0078 Pa) -> swamps the millipascal acoustic residual (proofs/v014/fp32_acoustic_probes.json). Needs perturbation-authoritative plumbing (ADR-031, large).
fp32_acoustic_refutation.verdict: the v0.15 strong lead (fp32 acoustic) is BOTH the wrong lever (tiny share) AND hard-blocked (cancellation). Confirmed, not assumed.

bottom_line.reason: The step is LAUNCH-BOUND (~14000 kernel launches/step, 159 ms/step launch API, GPU ~90% idle). The dominant cost is MYNN-EDMF (88 ms, 87%), not the fp64 acoustic dycore (10 ms). Precision (fp32) does not reduce the launch count and gives only ~1.16x even on the dycore; column tiling HURTS (more launches); fp32-acoustic is both tiny-share and cancellation-blocked.
bottom_line.identity_safe_win_shipped: fp32-BouLac (GPUWRF_MYNN_BOULAC_FP32, default OFF): MEASURED 1.20x on the isolated MYNN step (~8.5 ms/step), prognostic identity ~1.9e-10 rel (theta) -- far inside the frozen-tolerance gate. In the full forecast this is ~1.05x (the launch-bound ceiling caps it). A genuine, modest, identity-preserving wall-clock win, opt-in pending the operational field gate.
bottom_line.the_only_path_to_a_large_win: reduce the KERNEL/LAUNCH COUNT, dominated by MYNN-EDMF: (1) fuse the dense (B,nz,nz) BouLac elementwise chain into fewer larger kernels, and/or (2) unroll/restructure the EDMF per-level lax.scan (NUP=8 vmap x ~44 levels) so XLA fuses across levels (cf. the bit-identical Thomas unroll=45 -> 3.3x). This is a STRUCTURAL MYNN rewrite (in the mynn_edmf/mynn_pbl file domain Fable owns this sprint), not a precision or tiling change.
bottom_line.honest_ceiling: Even fully fixing MYNN (88->~20 ms if fusion lands) -> step ~110 ms -> ~1.6x vs CPU; halving it -> ~134 ms -> ~1.33x. fp32-BouLac alone is ~1.05x. A 5-10x is NOT reachable on this consumer card with the fully-WRF-faithful per-step kernel count -- the launch tax of ~14000 dependent kernels/step is the wall, and CUDA-graph capture / whole-step fusion would be required to break it (a large, risky XLA/runtime effort). The principal's go/no-go: the path to even ~1.5-2x exists (MYNN kernel-count reduction) but is a structural rewrite with real risk; precision (the v0.15 strong lead) is refuted as the lever.
